---
title: "WGCNA CortexCode ATAC, H3K27ac, H3K27me3"
author: "Karol Cichewicz"
date: "2026-04-30"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---


Optimal SPs were determined earlier

power_atac = 6
power_ac = 5
power_me3 = 9


```{r}
# ============================================================
# CortexCode WGCNA analysis workflow
# ============================================================
#
# This script documents the WGCNA workflow used to identify coordinated
# chromatin-accessibility and histone-modification modules in the CortexCode
# developmental epigenomic datasets.
#
# The script runs three related analyses:
#
#   1. ATAC-seq
#      - Input: IDR ATAC peak count matrix
#      - Peaks are already represented as uniform summit-centered intervals
#      - Counts are CPM-normalized
#      - WGCNA is run with softPower = 6
#      - Output: net_WGCNA_ATAC_sp6.Rds
#
#   2. H3K27ac ChIP-seq
#      - Input: H3K27ac merged peak count matrix
#      - Closely spaced intervals are iteratively merged if separated by
#        less than 1 kb
#      - Counts from original peaks mapping to each merged interval are summed
#      - Counts are CPM-normalized
#      - WGCNA is run with softPower = 5
#      - Output: net_WGCNA_H3K27ac_merged_by_1kb_sp5.Rds
#
#   3. H3K27me3 ChIP-seq
#      - Input: H3K27me3 merged peak count matrix
#      - Closely spaced intervals are iteratively merged if separated by
#        less than 1 kb
#      - Counts from original peaks mapping to each merged interval are summed
#      - Counts are CPM-normalized
#      - WGCNA is run with softPower = 9
#      - Output: net_WGCNA_H3K27me3_merged_by_1kb_sp9.Rds
#
# For H3K27ac and H3K27me3, the additional 1 kb merging step was used because
# histone-mark peak calls can be closely spaced or partially redundant,
# especially for broad or semi-broad chromatin domains. The merge step reduces
# redundancy by collapsing neighboring intervals into larger regulatory regions
# before module detection. Importantly, the merged interval counts are generated
# by summing the original peak-level counts, not by recalling peaks or recounting
# reads from BAM files.
#
# For all three modalities, WGCNA is performed on CPM-normalized count matrices
# with samples as rows and genomic intervals as columns. The same core WGCNA
# settings are used across datasets:
#
#   - corType = "bicor"
#   - networkType = "signed"
#   - TOMType = "signed"
#   - minModuleSize = 30
#   - maxBlockSize = 25000
#   - mergeCutHeight = 0.25
#   - deepSplit = 2
#   - pamStage = FALSE
#   - reassignThreshold = 1e-10
#   - saveTOMs = FALSE
#
# The script also includes QC and diagnostic plots at key steps:
#
#   - peak overlap distributions
#   - peak or merged-interval width distributions
#   - nearest-neighbor distance distributions
#   - total count distributions per sample
#   - per-peak/per-interval count distributions
#   - sample clustering dendrograms
#   - soft-threshold diagnostic plots
#   - WGCNA module dendrograms with module color annotations
#   - module size barplots
#
# Soft-threshold diagnostic plots are retained for transparency, but the final
# soft powers are fixed to the values used in the CortexCode analysis:
#
#   - ATAC-seq:    softPower = 6
#   - H3K27ac:     softPower = 5
#   - H3K27me3:    softPower = 9
#
# These values are treated as the final analysis parameters rather than
# re-optimized in this cleaned repository script.
#
# ============================================================

```

# ATAC

```{r}
# ATAC-seq WGCNA analysis for CortexCode
# Input: IDR ATAC peak count matrix (uniform peaks, mm10)
# Output: net_WGCNA_ATAC_sp6.Rds

# ============================================================
# 1. Setup
# ============================================================

library(WGCNA)
library(edgeR)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(tidyverse)

options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
enableWGCNAThreads(nThreads = 12)

repo_dir <- "C:/Users/karol/Box/Nord_Lab_Computational_Projects3/CortexCode/GitHub_repository"
setwd(repo_dir)

input_file  <- "WGCNA_input_data/atac_idr_peaks_merged_uniform-mm10.tsv"
output_file <- "WGCNA_objects/net_WGCNA_ATAC_sp6.Rds"

# ============================================================
# 2. Load ATAC peak count matrix
# ============================================================

data_atac <- read.table(input_file, header = TRUE)

# Create a unique peak ID from genomic coordinates
data_atac$ID <- paste0(data_atac$chrom, "_", data_atac$start, "_", data_atac$end)
rownames(data_atac) <- data_atac$ID

# Columns 5-24 are sample count columns; columns 1-4 are chrom, start, end, summit
count_cols <- 5:24
count_mat  <- data_atac[, count_cols]

# ============================================================
# 3. QC: peak overlap, width, and distance distributions
# ============================================================

gr_atac <- GRanges(
  seqnames = data_atac$chrom,
  ranges   = IRanges(start = data_atac$start, end = data_atac$end),
  summit   = data_atac$summit
)

# --- Peak overlap counts ---
# IDR peaks should be largely non-overlapping; flag any that are
overlaps <- findOverlaps(gr_atac)
overlaps <- overlaps[queryHits(overlaps) != subjectHits(overlaps)]  # exclude self-hits

overlap_counts <- table(queryHits(overlaps))
overlap_summary <- data.frame(
  Overlaps = 0:10,
  Count    = sapply(0:10, function(x) sum(overlap_counts == x))
)

ggplot(filter(overlap_summary, Overlaps <= 5),
       aes(x = Overlaps, y = Count)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), vjust = -0.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:5) +
  labs(
    title = paste("ATAC peak overlap distribution; n =", nrow(data_atac)),
    x     = "Number of overlapping intervals",
    y     = "Number of intervals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# --- Peak width distribution ---
width_data <- data.frame(Width = width(gr_atac))

ggplot(width_data, aes(x = Width)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10, fill = "skyblue", color = "skyblue") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 3000)) +
  labs(
    title = paste(
      "ATAC peak width distribution",
      "\nMedian:", median(width_data$Width),
      "  Min:", min(width_data$Width),
      "  Max:", max(width_data$Width)
    ),
    x = "Peak width (bp)",
    y = "Number of peaks"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# --- Distance to nearest peak ---
# Helps confirm peaks are well-separated and not redundant
nearest_distances <- distanceToNearest(gr_atac)
nearest_distances <- nearest_distances[
  queryHits(nearest_distances) != subjectHits(nearest_distances)
]

distance_data <- data.frame(Distance = mcols(nearest_distances)$distance)

ggplot(distance_data, aes(x = Distance)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 100, fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 50000), ylim = c(0, 750)) +
  labs(
    title = "Distance to nearest ATAC peak",
    x     = "Distance to nearest interval (bp)",
    y     = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ============================================================
# 4. QC: sample count distributions
# ============================================================

# Total library size per sample
total_counts <- colSums(count_mat)

df_total_counts <- data.frame(
  Sample   = names(total_counts),
  Counts   = total_counts,
  Counts_M = round(total_counts / 1e5) / 10  # label in millions, 1 decimal
)

ggplot(df_total_counts, aes(x = Sample, y = Counts)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Counts_M), vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Total ATAC counts per sample",
    x     = "",
    y     = "Total counts"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))

# Per-peak count distribution per sample on log scale
df_long <- melt(count_mat)

ggplot(df_long, aes(x = variable, y = log10(value + 1))) +
  geom_violin(fill = "lightgreen") +
  labs(
    title = "ATAC count distribution per sample",
    x     = "",
    y     = "log10(counts + 1)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))

# ============================================================
# 5. Construct sample metadata
# ============================================================

# Metadata is parsed from sample name conventions:
# age: embryonic (E##) or postnatal (P##)
# region: Caudal (_C) or Rostral (all others)
# dev: cell/layer type based on Cre driver or population label
metadata <- data.frame(Sample = colnames(count_mat)) %>%
  mutate(
    age    = str_extract(Sample, "E\\d+|P\\d+"),
    region = ifelse(str_detect(Sample, "_C"), "Caudal", "Rostral"),
    dev    = case_when(
      str_detect(Sample, "_Nex")  ~ "Neurons",
      str_detect(Sample, "_Vz")   ~ "VZ",
      str_detect(Sample, "_Svz")  ~ "SVZ",
      str_detect(Sample, "_Ntsr") ~ "L6",
      str_detect(Sample, "_Rbp")  ~ "L5",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

metadata

# ============================================================
# 6. CPM normalization and WGCNA input preparation
# ============================================================

# CPM normalizes for differences in library size across samples
dge     <- DGEList(counts = as.matrix(count_mat))
cpm_mat <- cpm(dge)

# WGCNA expects samples as rows and peaks as columns
datExpr <- t(cpm_mat)

# Remove peaks or samples that are missing or have zero variance
gsg <- goodSamplesGenes(datExpr, verbose = 3)
if (!gsg$allOK) {
  datExpr <- datExpr[gsg$goodSamples, gsg$goodGenes]
}

# Hierarchical clustering of samples — visually confirms expected groupings
# and helps identify outlier samples before running WGCNA
sampleTree <- hclust(dist(datExpr), method = "average")
plot(sampleTree, main = "ATAC sample clustering", sub = "", xlab = "")

# ============================================================
# 7. Soft-threshold power selection
# ============================================================

# Candidate powers to evaluate for scale-free topology fit
powers <- c(1:10, seq(12, 20, by = 2))

sft <- pickSoftThreshold(
  datExpr,
  powerVector = powers,
  verbose     = 5
)

par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# Scale-free topology fit: look for the lowest power that reaches R^2 >= 0.8
plot(
  sft$fitIndices[, 1],
  -sign(sft$fitIndices[, 3]) * sft$fitIndices[, 2],
  type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue",
  xlab = "Soft-threshold power",
  ylab = "Scale-free topology fit, signed R^2",
  main = "Scale independence"
)
abline(h = 0.8, col = "red", lty = 2)
text(
  sft$fitIndices[, 1],
  -sign(sft$fitIndices[, 3]) * sft$fitIndices[, 2],
  labels = powers, cex = 0.8
)

# Mean connectivity: should drop smoothly; avoid powers where it plateaus high
plot(
  sft$fitIndices[, 1],
  sft$fitIndices[, 5],
  type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue",
  xlab = "Soft-threshold power",
  ylab = "Mean connectivity",
  main = "Mean connectivity"
)
text(
  sft$fitIndices[, 1],
  sft$fitIndices[, 5],
  labels = powers, cex = 0.8
)

# ============================================================
# 8. Run WGCNA
# ============================================================

# sp=6 was selected based on soft-threshold diagnostics
softPower <- 6

net_WGCNA_ATAC_sp6 <- blockwiseModules(
  datExpr          = as.data.frame(datExpr),
  checkMissingData = TRUE,
  power            = softPower,
  networkType      = "signed",   # signed: distinguishes positive/negative correlations
  TOMType          = "signed",
  corType          = "bicor",    # biweight midcorrelation: robust to outliers
  minModuleSize    = 30,
  maxBlockSize     = 25000,
  mergeCutHeight   = 0.25,       # merge modules with >75% eigengene correlation
  deepSplit        = 2,          # moderate sensitivity for module splitting
  pamStage         = FALSE,
  reassignThreshold = 1e-10,
  saveTOMs         = FALSE,
  nThreads         = 12,
  verbose          = 3
)

saveRDS(net_WGCNA_ATAC_sp6, file = output_file)

# ============================================================
# 9. WGCNA module diagnostic plots
# ============================================================

module_colors <- labels2colors(net_WGCNA_ATAC_sp6$colors)

# Dendrogram with module color bar — confirms modules are well-separated clusters
plotDendroAndColors(
  net_WGCNA_ATAC_sp6$dendrograms[[1]],
  module_colors[net_WGCNA_ATAC_sp6$blockGenes[[1]]],
  "Module colors",
  dendroLabels = FALSE,
  hang         = 0.03,
  addGuide     = TRUE,
  guideHang    = 0.05,
  main         = "ATAC WGCNA peak dendrogram and module colors"
)

# Module size barplot — grey module contains unassigned peaks
module_size_df <- data.frame(
  Module = names(table(module_colors)),
  Size   = as.numeric(table(module_colors))
)

ggplot(module_size_df, aes(x = reorder(Module, Size), y = Size)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "ATAC WGCNA module sizes",
    x     = "Module",
    y     = "Number of ATAC peaks"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```



# AC

```{r}

# H3K27ac WGCNA analysis for CortexCode
# Input: H3K27ac peak count matrix
# Processing: iterative 1 kb peak merging, summed counts
# Output: net_WGCNA_H3K27ac_merged_by_1kb_sp5.Rds

# ============================================================
# 1. Setup
# ============================================================

library(WGCNA)
library(edgeR)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(parallel)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(tidyverse)
library(cowplot)

options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

repo_dir <- "C:/Users/karol/Box/Nord_Lab_Computational_Projects3/CortexCode/GitHub_repository"
setwd(repo_dir)

input_file  <- "WGCNA_input_data/hac_overlap_peaks_merged-mm10.tsv"
merged_rds  <- "WGCNA_input_data/data_h3k27ac_merged_by_1kb.RData"
output_file <- "WGCNA_objects/net_WGCNA_H3K27ac_merged_by_1kb_sp5.Rds"

n_threads <- 10
enableWGCNAThreads(nThreads = n_threads)

# ============================================================
# 2. Load H3K27ac peak count matrix
# ============================================================

data_h3k27ac <- read.table(input_file, header = TRUE)

# Columns 1-3 are genomic coordinates (chrom, start, end);
# all remaining columns are per-sample counts
count_cols     <- 4:ncol(data_h3k27ac)
count_colnames <- colnames(data_h3k27ac)[count_cols]

data_h3k27ac[, count_cols] <- lapply(data_h3k27ac[, count_cols], as.numeric)

# ============================================================
# 3. Merge peaks within 1 kb and sum counts
# ============================================================

# H3K27ac peaks from overlapping peak calls can be closely spaced;
# iterative merging collapses peaks within 1 kb into single intervals
# to reduce redundancy before WGCNA
gr_ac <- makeGRangesFromDataFrame(
  data_h3k27ac,
  keep.extra.columns = TRUE
)

merge_peaks_iteratively <- function(gr, min_gapwidth = 1000) {
  # Repeatedly reduce until no further merging occurs
  repeat {
    merged_gr <- reduce(gr, min.gapwidth = min_gapwidth)
    if (length(merged_gr) == length(gr)) break
    gr <- merged_gr
  }
  merged_gr
}

merged_gr_ac <- merge_peaks_iteratively(gr_ac, min_gapwidth = 1000)

# Map original peaks to merged intervals and sum counts within each interval
overlaps_ac <- findOverlaps(gr_ac, merged_gr_ac)

cl <- makeCluster(14)
clusterExport(
  cl,
  c("gr_ac", "overlaps_ac", "count_colnames"),
  envir = environment()
)

merged_counts_ac <- parSapply(cl, seq_along(merged_gr_ac), function(i) {
  rows <- queryHits(overlaps_ac)[subjectHits(overlaps_ac) == i]
  colSums(as.data.frame(gr_ac)[rows, count_colnames, drop = FALSE])
})

stopCluster(cl)

final_gr_ac <- merged_gr_ac
mcols(final_gr_ac) <- as.data.frame(t(merged_counts_ac))

saveRDS(final_gr_ac, file = merged_rds)

# ============================================================
# 4. QC: peak counts, widths, and distances before and after merge
# ============================================================

cat("Original H3K27ac peaks:          ", length(gr_ac), "\n")
cat("1 kb merged H3K27ac intervals:   ", length(final_gr_ac), "\n")

# Helper: compute distance-to-nearest and width for a GRanges object
make_distance_width_df <- function(gr, label) {
  nearest <- distanceToNearest(gr)
  nearest <- nearest[queryHits(nearest) != subjectHits(nearest)]  # exclude self-hits
  data.frame(
    Dataset  = label,
    Distance = mcols(nearest)$distance,
    Width    = width(gr)
  )
}

qc_before <- make_distance_width_df(gr_ac,       "Before 1 kb merge")
qc_after  <- make_distance_width_df(final_gr_ac, "After 1 kb merge")

qc_distance_width <- bind_rows(qc_before, qc_after)

# Summarize width and distance stats per dataset for plot subtitles
qc_stats <- qc_distance_width %>%
  group_by(Dataset) %>%
  summarise(
    med_dist  = median(Distance),
    min_dist  = min(Distance),
    max_dist  = max(Distance),
    med_width = median(Width),
    min_width = min(Width),
    max_width = max(Width),
    n         = n(),
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

# --- Distance to nearest interval: before vs after ---
# After merging, the minimum distance between intervals should increase,
# confirming that sub-1kb neighbors were successfully collapsed
dist_labels <- setNames(
  paste0(
    qc_stats$Dataset,
    "\nMedian: ", qc_stats$med_dist,
    " bp  |  Min: ", qc_stats$min_dist,
    "  Max: ", qc_stats$max_dist,
    "  n = ", qc_stats$n
  ),
  qc_stats$Dataset
)

ggplot(qc_distance_width, aes(x = Distance)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10, fill = "skyblue", color = "skyblue") +
  facet_wrap(~ Dataset, ncol = 1, labeller = as_labeller(dist_labels)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5000)) +
  labs(
    title = "H3K27ac distance to nearest interval before and after 1 kb merge",
    x     = "Distance to nearest interval (bp)",
    y     = "Number of intervals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# --- Interval width: before vs after ---
# After merging, widths should increase and the distribution shift right
width_labels <- setNames(
  paste0(
    qc_stats$Dataset,
    "\nMedian: ", qc_stats$med_width,
    " bp  |  Min: ", qc_stats$min_width,
    "  Max: ", qc_stats$max_width,
    "  n = ", qc_stats$n
  ),
  qc_stats$Dataset
)

ggplot(qc_distance_width, aes(x = Width)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10, fill = "skyblue", color = "skyblue") +
  facet_wrap(~ Dataset, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = as_labeller(width_labels)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 10000)) +
  labs(
    title = "H3K27ac interval width before and after 1 kb merge",
    x     = "Interval width (bp)",
    y     = "Number of intervals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ============================================================
# 5. Prepare merged count matrix
# ============================================================

# Flatten GRanges to data frame and use coordinate-based row IDs
data <- as.data.frame(final_gr_ac)

data$ID      <- paste0(data$seqnames, "_", data$start, "_", data$end)
rownames(data) <- data$ID

# Drop GRanges metadata columns; keep only count columns
data[c("ID", "seqnames", "start", "end", "width", "strand")] <- NULL

count_mat <- data[, count_colnames]

# ============================================================
# 6. QC: sample count distributions
# ============================================================

# Total library size per sample (labels shown in millions)
total_counts <- colSums(count_mat)

df_total_counts <- data.frame(
  Sample   = names(total_counts),
  Counts   = total_counts,
  Counts_M = round(total_counts / 1e5) / 10
)

ggplot(df_total_counts, aes(x = Sample, y = Counts)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Counts_M), vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Total H3K27ac counts per sample",
    x     = "",
    y     = "Total counts"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))

# Per-interval count distribution per sample on log scale
df_long <- melt(count_mat)

ggplot(df_long, aes(x = variable, y = log10(value + 1))) +
  geom_violin(fill = "lightgreen") +
  labs(
    title = "H3K27ac count distribution per sample",
    x     = "",
    y     = "log10(counts + 1)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))

# ============================================================
# 7. Construct sample metadata
# ============================================================

# Metadata is parsed from sample name conventions:
# age: embryonic (E##) or postnatal (P##)
# region: Caudal (_C) or Rostral (all others)
# dev: cell/layer type based on Cre driver or population label
metadata <- data.frame(Sample = colnames(count_mat)) %>%
  mutate(
    age    = str_extract(Sample, "E\\d+|P\\d+"),
    region = ifelse(str_detect(Sample, "_C"), "Caudal", "Rostral"),
    dev    = case_when(
      str_detect(Sample, "_Nex")  ~ "Neurons",
      str_detect(Sample, "_Vz")   ~ "VZ",
      str_detect(Sample, "_Svz")  ~ "SVZ",
      str_detect(Sample, "_Ntsr") ~ "L6",
      str_detect(Sample, "_Rbp")  ~ "L5",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

metadata

# ============================================================
# 8. CPM normalization and WGCNA input preparation
# ============================================================

# CPM normalizes for differences in library size across samples
dge     <- DGEList(counts = as.matrix(count_mat))
cpm_mat <- cpm(dge)

# WGCNA expects samples as rows and genomic intervals as columns
datExpr <- t(cpm_mat)

# Remove intervals or samples that are missing or have zero variance
gsg <- goodSamplesGenes(datExpr, verbose = 3)
if (!gsg$allOK) {
  datExpr <- datExpr[gsg$goodSamples, gsg$goodGenes]
}

# Hierarchical clustering of samples — visually confirms expected groupings
# and helps identify outlier samples before running WGCNA
sampleTree <- hclust(dist(datExpr), method = "average")
plot(sampleTree, main = "H3K27ac sample clustering", sub = "", xlab = "")

# ============================================================
# 9. Soft-threshold power selection
# ============================================================

# Candidate powers to evaluate for scale-free topology fit
powers <- c(1:10, seq(12, 20, by = 2))

sft <- pickSoftThreshold(
  datExpr,
  powerVector = powers,
  verbose     = 5
)

par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# Scale-free topology fit: look for the lowest power that reaches R^2 >= 0.8
plot(
  sft$fitIndices[, 1],
  -sign(sft$fitIndices[, 3]) * sft$fitIndices[, 2],
  type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue",
  xlab = "Soft-threshold power",
  ylab = "Scale-free topology fit, signed R^2",
  main = "Scale independence"
)
abline(h = 0.8, col = "red", lty = 2)
text(
  sft$fitIndices[, 1],
  -sign(sft$fitIndices[, 3]) * sft$fitIndices[, 2],
  labels = powers, cex = 0.8
)

# Mean connectivity: should drop smoothly; avoid powers where it plateaus high
plot(
  sft$fitIndices[, 1],
  sft$fitIndices[, 5],
  type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue",
  xlab = "Soft-threshold power",
  ylab = "Mean connectivity",
  main = "Mean connectivity"
)
text(
  sft$fitIndices[, 1],
  sft$fitIndices[, 5],
  labels = powers, cex = 0.8
)

# ============================================================
# 10. Run H3K27ac WGCNA at sp5
# ============================================================

# sp=5 was selected based on soft-threshold diagnostics above
softPower <- 5

net_WGCNA_H3K27ac_merged_by_1kb_sp5 <- blockwiseModules(
  datExpr           = as.data.frame(datExpr),
  checkMissingData  = TRUE,
  power             = softPower,
  networkType       = "signed",   # signed: distinguishes positive/negative correlations
  TOMType           = "signed",
  corType           = "bicor",    # biweight midcorrelation: robust to outliers
  minModuleSize     = 30,
  maxBlockSize      = 25000,
  mergeCutHeight    = 0.25,       # merge modules with >75% eigengene correlation
  deepSplit         = 2,          # moderate sensitivity for module splitting
  pamStage          = FALSE,
  reassignThreshold = 1e-10,
  saveTOMs          = FALSE,
  nThreads          = n_threads,
  verbose           = 3
)

saveRDS(net_WGCNA_H3K27ac_merged_by_1kb_sp5, file = output_file)

# ============================================================
# 11. WGCNA module diagnostic plots
# ============================================================

module_colors <- labels2colors(net_WGCNA_H3K27ac_merged_by_1kb_sp5$colors)

# Dendrogram with module color bar — confirms modules are well-separated clusters
plotDendroAndColors(
  net_WGCNA_H3K27ac_merged_by_1kb_sp5$dendrograms[[1]],
  module_colors[net_WGCNA_H3K27ac_merged_by_1kb_sp5$blockGenes[[1]]],
  "Module colors",
  dendroLabels = FALSE,
  hang         = 0.03,
  addGuide     = TRUE,
  guideHang    = 0.05,
  main         = "H3K27ac WGCNA peak dendrogram and module colors"
)

# Module size barplot — grey module contains unassigned intervals
module_size_df <- data.frame(
  Module = names(table(module_colors)),
  Size   = as.numeric(table(module_colors))
)

ggplot(module_size_df, aes(x = reorder(Module, Size), y = Size)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "H3K27ac WGCNA module sizes",
    x     = "Module",
    y     = "Number of H3K27ac intervals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```


# ME3

```{r}
# H3K27me3 WGCNA analysis for CortexCode
# Input: H3K27me3 peak count matrix
# Processing: iterative 1 kb peak merging, summed counts
# Output: net_WGCNA_H3K27me3_merged_by_1kb_sp9.Rds

# ============================================================
# 1. Setup
# ============================================================

library(WGCNA)
library(edgeR)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(parallel)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(tidyverse)
library(cowplot)

options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

repo_dir <- "C:/Users/karol/Box/Nord_Lab_Computational_Projects3/CortexCode/GitHub_repository"
setwd(repo_dir)

input_file  <- "WGCNA_input_data/htk_overlap_peaks_merged-mm10.tsv"
merged_rds  <- "WGCNA_input_data/data_h3k27me3_merged_by_1kb.RData"
output_file <- "WGCNA_objects/net_WGCNA_H3K27me3_merged_by_1kb_sp9.Rds"

n_threads <- 10
enableWGCNAThreads(nThreads = n_threads)

# ============================================================
# 2. Load H3K27me3 peak count matrix
# ============================================================

data_h3k27me3 <- read.table(input_file, header = TRUE)

# Columns 1-3 are genomic coordinates (chrom, start, end);
# all remaining columns are per-sample counts
count_cols     <- 4:ncol(data_h3k27me3)
count_colnames <- colnames(data_h3k27me3)[count_cols]

data_h3k27me3[, count_cols] <- lapply(data_h3k27me3[, count_cols], as.numeric)

# ============================================================
# 3. Merge peaks within 1 kb and sum counts
# ============================================================

# H3K27me3 is a broad mark; peaks from overlapping calls can be closely spaced.
# Iterative merging collapses peaks within 1 kb into single intervals
# to reduce redundancy before WGCNA
gr_me3 <- makeGRangesFromDataFrame(
  data_h3k27me3,
  keep.extra.columns = TRUE
)

merge_peaks_iteratively <- function(gr, min_gapwidth = 1000) {
  # Repeatedly reduce until no further merging occurs
  repeat {
    merged_gr <- reduce(gr, min.gapwidth = min_gapwidth)
    if (length(merged_gr) == length(gr)) break
    gr <- merged_gr
  }
  merged_gr
}

merged_gr_me3 <- merge_peaks_iteratively(gr_me3, min_gapwidth = 1000)

# Map original peaks to merged intervals and sum counts within each interval
overlaps_me3 <- findOverlaps(gr_me3, merged_gr_me3)

cl <- makeCluster(14)
clusterExport(
  cl,
  c("gr_me3", "overlaps_me3", "count_colnames"),
  envir = environment()
)

merged_counts_me3 <- parSapply(cl, seq_along(merged_gr_me3), function(i) {
  rows <- queryHits(overlaps_me3)[subjectHits(overlaps_me3) == i]
  colSums(as.data.frame(gr_me3)[rows, count_colnames, drop = FALSE])
})

stopCluster(cl)

final_gr_me3 <- merged_gr_me3
mcols(final_gr_me3) <- as.data.frame(t(merged_counts_me3))

saveRDS(final_gr_me3, file = merged_rds)

# ============================================================
# 4. QC: peak counts, widths, and distances before and after merge
# ============================================================

cat("Original H3K27me3 peaks:        ", length(gr_me3), "\n")
cat("1 kb merged H3K27me3 intervals: ", length(final_gr_me3), "\n")

# Helper: compute distance-to-nearest and width for a GRanges object
make_distance_width_df <- function(gr, label) {
  nearest <- distanceToNearest(gr)
  nearest <- nearest[queryHits(nearest) != subjectHits(nearest)]  # exclude self-hits
  data.frame(
    Dataset  = label,
    Distance = mcols(nearest)$distance,
    Width    = width(gr)
  )
}

qc_before <- make_distance_width_df(gr_me3,       "Before 1 kb merge")
qc_after  <- make_distance_width_df(final_gr_me3, "After 1 kb merge")

qc_distance_width <- bind_rows(qc_before, qc_after)

# Summarize width and distance stats per dataset for plot subtitles
qc_stats <- qc_distance_width %>%
  group_by(Dataset) %>%
  summarise(
    med_dist  = median(Distance),
    min_dist  = min(Distance),
    max_dist  = max(Distance),
    med_width = median(Width),
    min_width = min(Width),
    max_width = max(Width),
    n         = n(),
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

# --- Distance to nearest interval: before vs after ---
# After merging, the minimum distance between intervals should increase,
# confirming that sub-1kb neighbors were successfully collapsed
dist_labels <- setNames(
  paste0(
    qc_stats$Dataset,
    "\nMedian: ", qc_stats$med_dist,
    " bp  |  Min: ", qc_stats$min_dist,
    "  Max: ", qc_stats$max_dist,
    "  n = ", qc_stats$n
  ),
  qc_stats$Dataset
)

ggplot(qc_distance_width, aes(x = Distance)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10, fill = "skyblue", color = "skyblue") +
  facet_wrap(~ Dataset, ncol = 1, labeller = as_labeller(dist_labels)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5000)) +
  labs(
    title = "H3K27me3 distance to nearest interval before and after 1 kb merge",
    x     = "Distance to nearest interval (bp)",
    y     = "Number of intervals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# --- Interval width: before vs after ---
# After merging, widths should increase and the distribution shift right;
# particularly pronounced for H3K27me3 given its broad domain structure
width_labels <- setNames(
  paste0(
    qc_stats$Dataset,
    "\nMedian: ", qc_stats$med_width,
    " bp  |  Min: ", qc_stats$min_width,
    "  Max: ", qc_stats$max_width,
    "  n = ", qc_stats$n
  ),
  qc_stats$Dataset
)

ggplot(qc_distance_width, aes(x = Width)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10, fill = "skyblue", color = "skyblue") +
  facet_wrap(~ Dataset, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = as_labeller(width_labels)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 10000)) +
  labs(
    title = "H3K27me3 interval width before and after 1 kb merge",
    x     = "Interval width (bp)",
    y     = "Number of intervals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ============================================================
# 5. Prepare merged count matrix
# ============================================================

# Flatten GRanges to data frame and use coordinate-based row IDs
data <- as.data.frame(final_gr_me3)

data$ID        <- paste0(data$seqnames, "_", data$start, "_", data$end)
rownames(data) <- data$ID

# Drop GRanges metadata columns; keep only count columns
data[c("ID", "seqnames", "start", "end", "width", "strand")] <- NULL

count_mat <- data[, count_colnames]

# ============================================================
# 6. QC: sample count distributions
# ============================================================

# Total library size per sample (labels shown in millions)
total_counts <- colSums(count_mat)

df_total_counts <- data.frame(
  Sample   = names(total_counts),
  Counts   = total_counts,
  Counts_M = round(total_counts / 1e5) / 10
)

ggplot(df_total_counts, aes(x = Sample, y = Counts)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Counts_M), vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Total H3K27me3 counts per sample",
    x     = "",
    y     = "Total counts"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))

# Per-interval count distribution per sample on log scale
df_long <- melt(count_mat)

ggplot(df_long, aes(x = variable, y = log10(value + 1))) +
  geom_violin(fill = "lightgreen") +
  labs(
    title = "H3K27me3 count distribution per sample",
    x     = "",
    y     = "log10(counts + 1)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))

# ============================================================
# 7. Construct sample metadata
# ============================================================

# Metadata is parsed from sample name conventions:
# age: embryonic (E##) or postnatal (P##)
# region: Caudal (_C) or Rostral (all others)
# dev: cell/layer type based on Cre driver or population label
metadata <- data.frame(Sample = colnames(count_mat)) %>%
  mutate(
    age    = str_extract(Sample, "E\\d+|P\\d+"),
    region = ifelse(str_detect(Sample, "_C"), "Caudal", "Rostral"),
    dev    = case_when(
      str_detect(Sample, "_Nex")  ~ "Neurons",
      str_detect(Sample, "_Vz")   ~ "VZ",
      str_detect(Sample, "_Svz")  ~ "SVZ",
      str_detect(Sample, "_Ntsr") ~ "L6",
      str_detect(Sample, "_Rbp")  ~ "L5",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

metadata

# ============================================================
# 8. CPM normalization and WGCNA input preparation
# ============================================================

# CPM normalizes for differences in library size across samples
dge     <- DGEList(counts = as.matrix(count_mat))
cpm_mat <- cpm(dge)

# WGCNA expects samples as rows and genomic intervals as columns
datExpr <- t(cpm_mat)

# Remove intervals or samples that are missing or have zero variance
gsg <- goodSamplesGenes(datExpr, verbose = 3)
if (!gsg$allOK) {
  datExpr <- datExpr[gsg$goodSamples, gsg$goodGenes]
}

# Hierarchical clustering of samples — visually confirms expected groupings
# and helps identify outlier samples before running WGCNA
sampleTree <- hclust(dist(datExpr), method = "average")
plot(sampleTree, main = "H3K27me3 sample clustering", sub = "", xlab = "")

# ============================================================
# 9. Soft-threshold power selection
# ============================================================

# Candidate powers to evaluate for scale-free topology fit
powers <- c(1:10, seq(12, 20, by = 2))

sft <- pickSoftThreshold(
  datExpr,
  powerVector = powers,
  verbose     = 5
)

par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# Scale-free topology fit: look for the lowest power that reaches R^2 >= 0.8
plot(
  sft$fitIndices[, 1],
  -sign(sft$fitIndices[, 3]) * sft$fitIndices[, 2],
  type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue",
  xlab = "Soft-threshold power",
  ylab = "Scale-free topology fit, signed R^2",
  main = "Scale independence"
)
abline(h = 0.8, col = "red", lty = 2)
text(
  sft$fitIndices[, 1],
  -sign(sft$fitIndices[, 3]) * sft$fitIndices[, 2],
  labels = powers, cex = 0.8
)

# Mean connectivity: should drop smoothly; avoid powers where it plateaus high
plot(
  sft$fitIndices[, 1],
  sft$fitIndices[, 5],
  type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue",
  xlab = "Soft-threshold power",
  ylab = "Mean connectivity",
  main = "Mean connectivity"
)
text(
  sft$fitIndices[, 1],
  sft$fitIndices[, 5],
  labels = powers, cex = 0.8
)

# ============================================================
# 10. Run H3K27me3 WGCNA at sp9
# ============================================================

# sp=9 was selected based on soft-threshold diagnostics above;
# higher power than ATAC/H3K27ac reflects the broader, noisier
# signal structure typical of H3K27me3 domains
softPower <- 9

net_WGCNA_H3K27me3_merged_by_1kb_sp9 <- blockwiseModules(
  datExpr           = as.data.frame(datExpr),
  checkMissingData  = TRUE,
  power             = softPower,
  networkType       = "signed",   # signed: distinguishes positive/negative correlations
  TOMType           = "signed",
  corType           = "bicor",    # biweight midcorrelation: robust to outliers
  minModuleSize     = 30,
  maxBlockSize      = 25000,
  mergeCutHeight    = 0.25,       # merge modules with >75% eigengene correlation
  deepSplit         = 2,          # moderate sensitivity for module splitting
  pamStage          = FALSE,
  reassignThreshold = 1e-10,
  saveTOMs          = FALSE,
  nThreads          = n_threads,
  verbose           = 3
)

saveRDS(net_WGCNA_H3K27me3_merged_by_1kb_sp9, file = output_file)

# ============================================================
# 11. WGCNA module diagnostic plots
# ============================================================

module_colors <- labels2colors(net_WGCNA_H3K27me3_merged_by_1kb_sp9$colors)

# Dendrogram with module color bar — confirms modules are well-separated clusters
plotDendroAndColors(
  net_WGCNA_H3K27me3_merged_by_1kb_sp9$dendrograms[[1]],
  module_colors[net_WGCNA_H3K27me3_merged_by_1kb_sp9$blockGenes[[1]]],
  "Module colors",
  dendroLabels = FALSE,
  hang         = 0.03,
  addGuide     = TRUE,
  guideHang    = 0.05,
  main         = "H3K27me3 WGCNA peak dendrogram and module colors"
)

# Module size barplot — grey module contains unassigned intervals
module_size_df <- data.frame(
  Module = names(table(module_colors)),
  Size   = as.numeric(table(module_colors))
)

ggplot(module_size_df, aes(x = reorder(Module, Size), y = Size)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "H3K27me3 WGCNA module sizes",
    x     = "Module",
    y     = "Number of H3K27me3 intervals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```
